items.title: U.S. Coast Guard came to the rescue of Canadians stuck on ice
items.summary_auto: When a pair of Canadians got stranded on the ice on Lake St. Clair on Tuesday, an American team with a U.S. Coast Guard helicopter ended up coming to their rescue.

items.title: Carney government says India threat is over — Sikh activists say not so fast
items.summary_auto: The issuance of a duty to warn letter by Vancouver police and continued activity by the RCMP suggest that law enforcement are not convinced threats to Sikh activists have really subsided, even as the Carney government says Indian interference is a thing of the past.

items.title: Canada and South Korea sign a defence agreement
items.summary_auto: Canada and South Korea have signed a new defence agreement, roughly a month after Prime Minister Mark Carney urged middle powers to band together in the face of "great power" economic coercion.

items.title: I just watched the longest state of the union speech ever | About That
items.summary_auto: In Donald Trump's 2026 state of the union address — which broke the record for the longest such speech — he spoke about everything from lauding the men's Olympic hockey team to repeatedly chastizing Democrats for not standing up to applaud his comments. Andrew Chang breaks down the choreography of the address to explain the strategy behind the words. Images provided by The Canadian Press, Reuters and Getty Images

items.title: Power failures persist in Oji-Cree First Nation in northwestern Ontario despite repairs
items.summary_auto: Stanley Oskineegish says all of the infrastructure in his community has completely frozen. The chief of Nibinamik First Nation in northwestern Ontario has been busy co-ordinating Canadian Rangers and emergency supplies to support his people, since power outages have left homes and community buildings without heat. Here's the latest on the situation in the remote Oji-Cree community in northwestern Ontario.

items.title: 'What was so funny?': Hockey fans call out U.S. men's team for laughing at Trump's remark
Shrinking cold-water habitat means more whales get caught in nets, research finds
items.summary_auto: Humpback whales are more likely to get entangled in fishing gear in years when rising ocean temperatures make cold-water habitat harder to find, according to new research published Wednesday.

items.title: Harvey Weinstein hires Luigi Mangione's and Sean 'Diddy' Combs's lawyers
items.summary_auto:
Harvey Weinstein has hired Luigi Mangione and Sean "Diddy" Combs's lawyers to represent him at his third New
York rape trial, reshaping his legal team after declining to end the matter with a guilty plea. The lawyers, Jacob Kaplan, Marc Agnifilo and Teny Geragos, take over for Weinstein's longtime lawyer, Arthur Aidala, who ceded his courtroom role to focus on the ex-studio boss' appeals and pending civil matters.

items.title: Barred from NBA, ex-Raptor Jontay Porter signs with U.S. minor pro league team
items.summary_auto: Former Toronto Raptor Jontay Porter has signed with the Seattle SuperHawks for the 2026 season of the United States Basketball League, a minor pro league relaunching this spring with eight teams.

items.title: Democrats investigating whether U.S. DOJ withheld Epstein documents about abuse accusation against Trump
items.summary_auto: Democratic lawmakers on the House oversight and government reform committee promised on Tuesday to investigate allegations that the U.S. Department of Justice (DOJ) withheld material about an accusation against U.S. President Donald Trump from the Epstein files.

items.title: B.C. premier says Senate committee wants to gut bill that could close loopholes for extortion suspects
items.summary_auto: Premier David Eby says a Senate committee intends to gut key reforms to Canada's immigration laws. Eby had hoped reforms under Bill C-12 would stop extortion suspects from claiming refugee status to delay deportation.

items.title: Closing time at Diageo's Crown Royal plant in Ontario sees tears, hugs after workers' last shift
items.summary_auto: There were hugs, tears and frustration outside the Diageo Crown Royal facility in Amherstburg, Ont., as workers left the bottling plant for the last time Wednesday. Their final goodbye comes a few months after the England-based spirits giant announced it would close the plant, which opened in 1971 and is said to be the town's biggest employer.

items.title: Tears, hugs outside Crown Royal plant after Amherstburg, Ont., workers complete last shift
items.summary_auto: There were hugs, tears and frustration outside the Diageo Crown Royal facility in Amherstburg, Ont., as workers left the bottling plant for the last time Wednesday. Their final goodbye comes a few months after the England-based spirits giant announced it would close the plant, which opened in 1971 and is said to be the town's biggest employer.

items.title: 4 killed in shootout involving U.S.-registered boat, Cuban officials say
items.summary_auto: Cuba's government said Wednesday that its soldiers killed four people aboard a speedboat registered in Florida that opened fire on officers in Cuban waters. Cuba's Interior Ministry issued a statement that noted the boat was roughly 1.6 kilometres northeast of Cayo Falcones, off Cuba's north coast.

items.title: Hockey's Greg Westlake, nordic skier Mark Arendz, curler Ina Forrest front Canada's Paralympic team
items.summary_auto: Veteran Paralympians Greg Westlake, Adam Dixon, Mark Arendz and Ina Forrest will lead Canada's team at the Milano-Cortina Games.

items.title: Police arrest 2 teens, 30-year-old woman after spate of 'hoax' threats to Winnipeg schools
items.summary_auto: Winnipeg police arrested two teens and a 30-year-old woman in connection with three of the nearly a dozen threats made to city schools over a two-week span this month.

items.title: Hamilton police officer charged with possession of child sexual abuse material has history working with teens
items.summary_auto: An officer who has worked 19 years with the Hamilton Police Service has been charged with allegedly possessing and transmitting material that shows child sexual abuse and exploitation. He was arrested Wednesday, police said.

items.title: Astronaut evacuated from International Space Station pays tribute to 'incredible teammates'
items.summary_auto: Astronaut Mike Fincke has identified himself as the one who suffered a medical issue aboard the International Space Station last month, prompting NASA's first-ever medical evacuation.

items.title: Innu chief condemns 'cowboy' tactics after wildlife officers raid cabin for caribou carcass
items.summary_auto: An Innu community on Quebec’s Lower North Shore is shaken by an intervention by wildlife officers who circled and attempted to enter a cabin in Ekuanitshit Monday evening, injuring an elderly woman in the process.

items.title: Kendamil baby formula sold on Amazon, Costco recalled due to toxin-related concerns
items.summary_auto: The Canadian Food Inspection Agency says a baby formula sold online and at Costco is being recalled due to concerns of a toxin that can cause food poisoning.

items.title: N.L. schools to re-open after closing for three days due to snow
items.summary_auto: After being closed for three days due to significant snowfall, schools in the St. John's area are re-opening on Thursday.

items.title: Phil Collins, Lauryn Hill, Shakira among 2026 Rock & Roll Hall of Fame nominees
items.summary_auto: Phil Collins, Mariah Carey, Lauryn Hill, INXS, Iron Maiden, Luther Vandross and Shakira are among the 2026 nominees for induction into the Rock & Roll Hall of Fame, a wide net that includes rap, metal, R&B, hip-hop, Britpop, blues rock and pop.

items.title: What a viral fake video of Ghislaine Maxwell in Quebec City says about AI deception
items.summary_auto: A video purporting to show Ghislaine Maxwell, the former girlfriend and longtime associate of Jeffrey Epstein, out for a stroll in Quebec City has gained millions of views since it was posted last week. It’s fake.

items.title: Woman became recluse, lost 'zest for life' after alleged sex assault by Frank Stronach, court hears
items.summary_auto: A woman who claims she was sexually assaulted by Frank Stronach back in 1986 said she became a social recluse after the alleged attack made her mistrust men for the rest of her life.

items.title: Doctor faces 43 sex crime charges, including voyeurism, sexual assault, child pornography
items.summary_auto: Physician David Edward-Ooi Poon, who made international headlines during the COVID-19 pandemic with his campaign to reunite cross-border families and couples, has been accused of a slew of crimes spanning the last 12 years and is suspended from his medical practices in Ontario and Saskatchewan.

items.title: Iran slams Trump's state of the union 'lies,' but heads back to the negotiating table with U.S.
items.summary_auto: Iran's negotiating delegation has left Tehran for a third ​round of nuclear talks with the U.S., ​state media reported on Wednesday, even as other Iranian officials slammed comments made by President Donald Trump during his state of the union address.

items.title: Resident Evil Requiem showcases the best — and silliest — of survival horror video games
items.summary_auto: There's more to nitpick about Resident Evil Requiem than some might have hoped, but the truth is it does so much so well that none of them stop it from being a must-play entrant in the series.

items.title: 1 dead, 4 injured after SUV crashes into Ottawa drop-in centre
items.summary_auto: One person was killed and four others injured when a vehicle crashed into a drop-in centre at the corner of Rideau and Cobourg streets in downtown Ottawa on Wednesday morning.

items.title: Hunter fined after pet dogs shot during Ontario coyote hunt, but owner says ‘justice has not been served’
items.summary_auto: The owner of two dogs that were shot dead during a hunting incident in Central Elgin in southwestern Ontario last year says a hefty fine and suspended licence is not enough to deter hunters from crossing into private property.

items.title: Relocated bison herd aims to bring food security to Métis in northern Alberta
items.summary_auto: Leaders of the Willow Lake Métis Nation located south of Fort McMurray, Alta., say the return of a wood bison herd to their traditional territory is a victory for food sustainability.

items.title: First signs of herring spawn spark excitement on Vancouver Island
items.summary_auto: The herring spawn is an auspicious time on the South Coast. For some coastal First Nations, it represents the coming of spring and a time to gather food, while for conservationists it’s a time to take stock of the fish’s numbers.

items.title: Bill Gates apologizes to foundation for Epstein ties, says he 'saw nothing illicit'
items.summary_auto: Bill Gates "took ​responsibility for his actions" over ties to late financier and sex offender Jeffrey Epstein in a town hall meeting with employees of the Gates Foundation, a spokesperson for the philanthropic group told Reuters in a written statement.

items.title: 3 modern classics by Black Canadian authors to read
items.summary_auto: Ryan B. Patrick appeared on The Next Chapter with Antonio Michael Downing to talk about books he considers modern Black classics.

items.title: At least 30 dead, dozens missing from floods, mudslides in southeastern Brazil
items.summary_auto: At least 30 people died after heavy rains hit Brazil's southeastern state of Minas Gerais, ‌local authorities said Tuesday, while 39 people were missing.

items.title: Looking for an affordable home? The mayor of Faro, Yukon, hopes to hear from you
items.summary_auto: While many people across the Yukon struggle to find affordable housing, the mayor of Faro says people should consider moving to his town.

items.title: ‘Representation is huge’: Regina’s 1st Black female officer hopes to blaze path for others
items.summary_auto: Desiree Ates, whose mother is Indigenous and whose father is Black, is the first Black female police officer at the Regina Police Service. Now, she hopes to bring her background and experiences to build relationships in her community.

items.title: Trump sticks to celebratory script in state of the union, with one angry detour
items.summary_auto: U.S. President Donald Trump delivered a state of the union address that, by his standards, came across as relatively focused and disciplined, giving a preview of how the Republicans may try to campaign to keep control of Congress in this fall's midterms.

items.title: Any trade deal with Canada will include tariffs, says Trump's trade rep
items.summary_auto: U.S. President Donald Trump's point man on trade talks says Canada needs to accept that tariffs will be a part of any deal with the administration, including renewal of the Canada-U.S.-Mexico Agreement.

items.title: Where is Canada's immigration minister? Community groups are asking
items.summary_auto: Newcomer organizations are expressing concerns about Immigration Minister Lena Diab's availability, and nine Liberal MPs are questioning her place at the cabinet table.

items.title: Former public servant says Phoenix payroll system 'ruined' his 20s and haunts him still
items.summary_auto: Jordan Morrison says his 20s were ruined by Phoenix pay errors that he's still fighting today. An economist warns the federal government will continue losing talented young workers to the private sector if these errors continue.

items.title: Canadian travellers with trips planned to Mexico weighing whether or not to stay home
items.summary_auto: While Canadian airlines started flying to and from Mexico again on Tuesday, caution is still advised in the region, leaving tourists trying to decide what to do. And unless travellers bought the right kind of insurance, they might not be able to get their money back if they want to cancel or rebook their vacations.

items.title: From James Van Der Beek to Eric Dane, what's behind the celebrity crowdfunding backlash
items.summary_auto: In what feels to some like a sea change in acceptable behaviour, celebrities and those close to them have been calling for everyday fans to contribute to grassroots fundraising efforts. Not everyone thinks that's OK.
